q:
know
 How long (you) ___ Katherine?
a: How long have you known Katherine?

q:
be
 How long (your sister) ___ in Australia?
a: How long has your sister been in Australia?

q:
teach
 How long (you) ___ English?
a: How long have you been teaching English?

q:
have
 How long (you) ___ that jacket?
a: How long have you had that jacket?

q:
work
 How long (Joe) ___ at the airport?
a: How long has Joe been working at the airport?

q:
live
 (you) ___ always ___ in Chicago?
a: Have you always lived in Chicago?

q:
see
 Do you see Lisa very often? No, I (not / see) ___ her for three months.
a: No, I haven't seen her for three months.

q:
be
 Is Paul married? Yes, he (be) ___ married for ten years.
a: Yes, he has been married for ten years.

q:
be
 Is Amy married? Yes, she (be) ___ married to a German guy.
a: Yes, she is married to a German guy.

q:
play
 Do you still play tennis? No, I (not / play) ___ tennis for years.
a: No, I haven't played tennis for years.

q:
wait
 Are you waiting for the bus? Yes, I (wait) ___ for about 20 minutes.
a: Yes, I've been waiting for about 20 minutes.

q:
know
 You know Mel, don't you? Yes, we (know) ___ each other a long time.
a: Yes, we've known each other a long time.

q:
be
 Jack is never ill, is he? No, he (be) ___ ill since I've known him.
a: No, he's been ill since I've known him.

q:
live
 Martin lives in Italy, doesn't he? Yes, he (live) ___ in Milan.
a: Yes, he lives in Milan.

q:
live
 Sue lives in Berlin, doesn't she? Yes, she (live) ___ in Berlin for many years.
a: Yes, she has been living in Berlin for many years.

q:
watch
 Is Joe watching TV? Yes, he (watch) ___ TV all evening.
a: Yes, he's been watching TV all evening.

q:
watch
 Do you watch TV a lot? No, I (not / watch) ___ TV since last weekend.
a: No, I haven't watched TV since last weekend.

q:
have
 Do you have a headache? Yes, I (have) ___ a headache all morning.
a: Yes, I've had a headache all morning.

q:
go
 Do you go to the cinema a lot? No, I (not / go) ___ to the cinema for ages.
a: No, I haven't been to the cinema for ages.

q:
always/want
 Would you like to go to New York one day? Yes, I (always / want) ___ to go to New York.
a: Yes, I've always wanted to go to New York.
